steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: The sentence lists three films in a series: Zoot Suit (1981), El Norte (1983), and Stand and Deliver (1988), attributed to three filmmakers respectively. Rule: items in a series of three must be separated by commas: item 1, item 2, and item 3. The blank must close Zoot Suit's year and open El Norte with a comma between them.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
A. (1981) *El Norte* (1983), — no comma after (1981), so Zoot Suit and El Norte run together without separation. Reconstruct: "*Zoot Suit* (1981) *El Norte* (1983), and *Stand and Deliver*" — the first two series items are not separated. Eliminated.

B. (1981) *El Norte* (1983) — no comma after (1981) and no comma after (1983), collapsing all three items together with no separation at all. Eliminated.

C. (1981)---*El Norte* (1983)--- — dashes set off El Norte as a parenthetical aside rather than a series item. Reconstruct: "*Zoot Suit* (1981)---*El Norte* (1983)---and *Stand and Deliver*" — the dash structure breaks the parallel series. Eliminated.

D. (1981), *El Norte* (1983), — comma after (1981) separates item 1 from item 2; comma after (1983) separates item 2 from item 3. Reconstruct: "*Zoot Suit* (1981), *El Norte* (1983), and *Stand and Deliver* (1988)" — correct series punctuation. Correct.
